---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-data_blue_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size55[
Auditing and exploring the second data project  
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Check the Canvas submissions

2. Update slides BEFORE class
    - Add data view for discussing observations
    - Add code checking for missing data by variable, region and year
    - Is it time to introduce rworldmap for checking regional missing data




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.content-box-white[**For Today: Data Project 2**]]

.size55[
1. **Audit**: How is the data organized? Are there missing data problems (time or region)?

2. **Explore**: Submit **THREE** interesting, puzzling or surprising things about the world of this dataset.
]

???

From Canvas

- Audit the Dataset: Make sure you understand how the data is organized (observations and variables), and have spent time auditing it for missing data (time or region)

- Explore the Dataset: Submit THREE interesting, puzzling or surprising things about the world of this dataset. Fully explain each of your findings and how you can support your claims using the data.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Show data
d |>
  filter(year == 2019) |>
  slice_sample(n = 10) |>
  kbl(digits = 2)
```

???

How is the data organized? 

<br>

### 1. How are the observations organized?

<br>

### 2. Do we understand all of the measures here and can we connect them back to the codebook?

<br>

### 3. What missing data issues did you identify?

### e.g. regional, temporal or related to specific measures?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Missing Data by Variable
# Method 1 - Numeric Variables
summary(?$?)

# Method 2 - Categorical or Numeric Variables
table(is.na(?$?))
```

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Missing Data by Variable Across Time
table(d$Year, is.na(d$Total))
```

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Missing Data by Variable by Region - Making a Map

# Example below using fragile states total index

# Install package (one time)
install.packages("rworldmap")

# Load package (every time)
library(rworldmap)

# Filter data to one year
d2023 <- filter(d, Year == 2023)

# Merge data to the map data
map_data_2023 <- joinCountryData2Map(dF = d2023, joinCode = "NAME", nameJoinColumn = "Country")

# Output the Map
mapCountryData(map_data_2023, nameColumnToPlot = "Total")

# Tweak the map
mapCountryData(map_data_2023, nameColumnToPlot = "Total", 
               mapTitle = "Fragile States Index (Total 2023)", 
               colourPalette = "heat",
               catMethod = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100, 125))

# catMethod = seq(0, 120, 20)
# catMethod = "quantiles"
```

???





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Map result to discuss


```

???

### Any obvious missing data issues here?

<br>

Split up years from the dataset and have each person in class make a different map so we can walk around and see them all

<br>

Then you show an animated gif of all maps



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Auditing Data Project 2**]]

```{r, echo = FALSE, fig.align = 'center', out.width = '90%'}
knitr::include_graphics("libs/Images/military_Spending.gif")
```

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Animate the map (gif)


```

???

```{r}
# Code to share with class

# # Packages
# library(tidyverse)
# library(readxl)
# library(rworldmap)
# library(gifski)
# 
# # Load across time data and save as 'd'
# 
# # Create and save separate maps by year using a for loop
# for (i in 2008:2020) {
# 
#   # Create a subset for each year
#   # Create a new categorical variable for missing data
#   d_repo <- filter(d, year ==  i) |>
#   mutate(
#     Missing = if_else(is.na(military), "Data Missing", "Data Available")
#   )
# 
#   # Join WDI data to map data
#   d_repo_map <- joinCountryData2Map(dF = d_repo, joinCode = "ISO3", nameJoinColumn = "country_code")
# 
#   # Save each map as a png file
#   png(str_c("map-", i, "-military.png"),
#       width = 850,
#       height = 600,
#       units = "px")
# 
#   mapCountryData(d_repo_map,
#                  nameColumnToPlot = "Missing",
#                  catMethod = "categorical",
#                  mapTitle = str_c("Military Spending: ", i),
#                  colourPalette = c("skyblue", "red"),
#                  borderCol = "black")
# 
#   dev.off()
# }
# 
# # Make a list of the saved map files
# png_files <- list.files(pattern = ".*military.png$", full.names = TRUE)
# 
# # Use gifski to combine the separate images
# gifski(png_files, 
#        gif_file = "libs/Images/military_Spending.gif", 
#        width = 850, 
#        height = 600, 
#        delay = 1)
```





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size45[.content-box-white[**Exploring Data Project 2**]]

???

### What did you find from your pre-class explorations?

- *Share interesting/puzzling/surprising findings*

- *ON BOARD*




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-slate_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-class: center
class: middle

.center[.size50[.content-box-blue[**Report 2: Analyzing our Predictor Variable(s)**]]]

<br>

.size45[
1. Why is this project important?

2. How confident should we be in the methodology?

3. What do the measures currently show us?

4. How are these measures changing across time?
]

???

Next week we'll write the second report!

Hopefully everyone has a bunch of material for the first two sections now.

Dive into the data analyses over the break!
